_readme: The reference platform's published values for the fourteen tickers — the nine of the
M1 gate (#44) plus one liquid representative per B3 setor econômico the nine lacked
(ABEV3, LREN3, HAPV3, TOTS3, VIVT3), so the calculator is measured outside the
Captured by hand: platforms are a moving target, so the platform, the URL and the
capture date are part of the record. Our side is NOT stored here; it is computed by
the real calculator from tests/fixtures/analysis_inputs.json (regenerate that with
scripts/export_reference_inputs.py). A divergence is therefore a failing test, not a
paragraph — and a divergence we accept has to be written down, cell by cell, with its
reason. Widening a tolerance to hide one is what this file exists to prevent.
Each `except` reason is a HYPOTHESIS about OUR basis, not a verdict about the platform
(ADR 0022). It leads with its bucket: (a) a different statement (parent vs consolidated);
(b) a different period or basis (TTM vs closed year; paid vs declared; accounting vs
adjusted; closing vs average equity); (c) a different share base (issued vs outstanding;
equity gross vs net of treasury); (d) our defect, shown arithmetically — none found; or
(platform-gap) the platform published no value or an unreconcilable one, shown by its own
numbers. The #100 audit (banks/insurers primary sources + AUVP + Investidor10, 2026-07-15)
classified every divergence here; its full working record is tmp/audit-reference/.

tolerances._why: Relative. A margin is arithmetic on two filed lines and must land exactly; a per-share value carries the share count, which moves with a corporate action mid-year; ROE divides by a closing balance (ADR 0003) whose basis the platform does not publish.

not_compared._why: Indicators whose divergence is decided, not accidental. Comparing them would only measure the platform's basis against ours.
not_compared.pe: The platform draws its historical multiples on the dividend-adjusted price series; we divide by what the shares actually traded at that year (ADR 0018). PETR4 2022 reads 0.91 there and 2.25 here, and a P/E under 1 is not how anyone valued Petrobras.
not_compared.roa: The platform publishes it rounded to two decimals of a fraction (0.01 for a bank), which carries no information to compare against.
not_compared.dividend_yield: (b) period/basis, both sides of the fraction. We divide the dividends the company actually *paid* in cash that year (the DFC) by that year's average market cap; the platform divides the dividends *declared* for the year, per share, by a single price. For PETR4 2024 the two land at 19.9% (ours) against 21.92% (theirs) — the same company, measured two ways. The gap is largest for heavy payers whose cash paid lags declarations (banks: BBDC4 declared ~62% payout for 2024, our cash-paid basis ~34%). The *declared* figure is already in the mirror (the DMPL, ADR 0016) and could be read if alignment is wanted (#100.1). What the gate does check is that no closed year yields more than the whole company is worth — the failure the adjusted-price basis used to produce (105.9% for PETR4 2022, ADR 0018).

except.eps: (c) share base: the platform divides by the shares VALE *issued*; we divide by the shares outstanding, net of the 270 M it holds in treasury (ADR 0017). The 4% gap is exactly that stake.
except.bvps: (c) share base: same treasury basis as eps (ADR 0017).

except.roe: (platform-gap): the platform publishes 0.00 for BBAS3 2024 book value per share, and its ROE for that year reconciles with no equity the bank reports. The arithmetic that makes it their hole, not our difference: its 2025 ROE reproduces from the filings (controllers' result over the average equity — see 2025), so their recipe is recoverable in 2025 while their 2024 cell reconciles with nothing.

(b) platform basis — the bank recipe differs from the corporate one: controllers' result over the AVERAGE controllers' equity reproduces its 0.0983 to 0.13% (0.09843), while ADR 0026's total-over-closing gives 0.0938, 4.6% apart. Matches #93/#100's finding that the platform's bank ROE fits the average balance; recorded per cell rather than special-casing banks in the gate.
except.bvps: (platform-gap): the platform's book value implies an equity ~2% below the R$189.2 bn BBAS3 files; its bank book-value column publishes 0.00 for 2024, so it is unreliable for this line. Recorded against the platform, re-checked against a second source in #93. (AUVP/Investidor10 agree with our VPA 33.45 on the TTM basis, corroborating our equity read.)

except.roe: (platform-gap): same hole as BBAS3 2024 — the platform publishes no book value per share, and its ROE implies an equity Bradesco does not report. Its 2025 ROE (14.25%) agrees with ours (13.76%), so the difference is their 2024 cell, not our basis.

except.bvps: (platform-gap): same unreliable bank book-value column as BBAS3 2025 — it implies an equity ~4% below the R$178.4 bn Bradesco files, and publishes 0.00 for 2024. Re-checked against a second source in #93.
except.roe: (platform-gap): follows its bvps — the same understated equity is this ROE's denominator.

except.eps: (c) share base: Dados de Mercado divides by the 1.941 bn shares BBSE3 issued; we divide by the 1.883 bn outstanding, net of the 58.8 M in treasury (ADR 0017). The 3% gap is that stake. (Its 2024 cell uses a post-buyback count and so agrees — a within-platform inconsistency across years.)
except.bvps: Two different effects, per period. vs Dados de Mercado (5.35) it is (c) share base: it divides our net-of-treasury 2025 equity by *issued* shares, we by outstanding — the same share-count effect as eps. vs AUVP/Investidor10 (6.51) it is (b) period: those show the *current* quarter (2026-03-31), where BBSE3 cancelled the R$1.869 bn of treasury it held at 2025-12-31 (2.03.04.09: −1.869 → −0.005 bn), so its filed 2.03 equity genuinely rose to R$12.64 bn. Our own TTM view reads that same balance and also yields VPA 6.51 — we match them like-for-like. Our closed-year 5.52 is the filed 2025 2.03 total ÷ outstanding, verified against the mirror (ADR 0017, 0022) — not a defect.

except.eps: (c) share base: a unit bundles 1 ON + 2 PN, so we divide by the number of units — the per-unit LPA that pairs with the unit price (R$3.07, reconciling exactly with the DRE's own per-class LPA at 3.99: 1×0.959 + 2×1.055). The platform publishes the per-*share* figure (1.02) against the per-*unit* price — the ~3× gap, and why its P/E reads ~25 against a real ~8 (#38).
except.bvps: (c) share base: same per-unit basis as eps — equity ÷ units (#38).

except.eps: (c) share base: HAPV3 executed a 15:1 reverse split (grupamento, approved 2025-04-30, trading adjusted 2025-06-06). We divide 2024 by the ~7.41 bn shares filed *for* 2024; the platform restates all history onto the current 502,630,884 issued (it publishes that single count for both years). ours/platform = 1/14.75 ≈ 1/15 net of treasury — the count, not the numerator (margins and ROE agree to 0.3%). Not a defect on either side: the as-filed vs restated decision is #76.
except.bvps: (c) share base — the same grupamento restatement as eps (#76).

except.eps: (c) share base: issued vs outstanding (ADR 0017) — the platform divides by the 502,630,884 issued; we by the ~471 M outstanding, net of treasury. The ~6% gap is that stake.
except.bvps: (c) share base — issued vs outstanding, as eps (ADR 0017).

except.eps: (c) share base: the whole gap is the count — eps and bvps carry the same ours/platform ratio within each year (0.956 in 2024, 1.024 in 2025) while margins and ROE agree to 0.02%. The direction *flips* between years, so no fixed treasury stake explains it; it fits the platform dividing every year by the *current* issued count (its HAPV3 page shows one count for all years; its BBSE3 2025 cell divides by issued) while we serve each year's outstanding as filed (ADR 0017). The as-filed vs restated choice is #76.
except.bvps: (c) share base — same count-only ratio as eps (#76).

except.eps: (c) share base — see 2024; the sign flips because 2025's outstanding sits below the current issued count the platform uses everywhere (#76).

except.gross_margin: (b) basis — cause UNKNOWN, recorded loudly: 1.19% apart against a 1% tolerance, while 2025 agrees to 0.01% with the same mapping, so the mapping is not the gap. Leading hypothesis, unverified: the platform reads 2024 from the 2025 filing's restated comparative (the 2025 DFP restates 2024 with Dimensa as a discontinued operation) while we read the 2024 filing as filed.
except.net_margin: (b) basis — the minority slice is no longer the gap (ADR 0026: 2025 agrees to 0.01% on the total basis), yet 2024 stays 5.9% apart even on the platform's own total basis (735,443/5,224,007 = 0.1408 vs its 0.1496). Same one-year-only shape as gross_margin, and the same leading hypothesis: the restated-comparative 2024, with Dimensa discontinued. Unverified.
except.eps: (c) share base: a ~2% count gap, barely over tolerance — the same current-issued vs as-filed-outstanding family as LREN3 (#76).
except.bvps: (c) share base: the '~9% equity numerator' this cell used to record was our #118 defect (a grandchild reserve line, 2.03.02.09, read as the minority block); fixed, what remains is eps's own ~2.3% count gap (585.9M ours vs ~599.4M implied) — the same family as eps (#76).

except.eps: (c) share base: per-unit LPA — TAEE11 bundles 1 ON + 2 PN (R$4.92 = 3 × the DRE's 3.99 per-share R$1.639); the platform publishes the per-share figure. See SAPR11 2024 (#38).
except.bvps: (c) share base: per-unit basis — equity ÷ units. See SAPR11 2024 (#38).
